element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Single-shot default weapon; player starts with this
Dual-input system: gun for air enemies, bombs for ground targets in vertical sections
Five-stage progression: normal → twin laser → penetration beam → three-way flame → Turtle Dove mode
First meaningful firepower improvement; increases speed
Mandatory transition after defeating guardian; introduces platforming with walking, crouching, climbing ladders, jumping
Giant mechanical face inhales player; must escape or face hazard; different boss from main levels
Hidden treasure chests for score gain; optional exploration reward
Mid-tier upgrade; projectiles pierce through targets
Advanced spread weapon; fourth power-up stage
Ultimate form with wide shots (4x default damage); can withstand 2 hits before downgrade
Up to 9 continues stockable; found in bonus/optional levels
NES-exclusive addition; appears at end of each vertical segment
Side-scroll combat; flight returns for final boss phase

typical_relief_triggers: Completing vertical segment and reaching palace
typical_tension_triggers: Loss of power-up after being hit
Vertical scroll section with dense airborne enemies
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Bipartite rhythm within each level: sustained tension in vertical section (aerial dodging, enemy density), moderate relief during palace navigation, escalating tension toward boss encounters. Five-area structure provides macro-level tension arc.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Danger area marked by giant mechanical face appearing mid-level; Lucky areas are visually hidden and reward exploration
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Lucky hidden treasure areas accessible from vertical-scrolling segments; require intentional off-path searching

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction to dual-input system (gun + bombs); vertical scrolling with manageable airborne enemies; basic platform mechanics in first palace
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Mandatory Danger area encounters introduce hazard avoidance; power-up progression accelerates; palace complexity increases with more combat
difficulty_curve.late_game: Dragon bosses in vertical segments; advanced power-ups available (three-way, Turtle Dove); final boss requiring sustained flight combat with ultimate weaponry
notable_spikes: First Danger area (mid-Level 1): sudden mandatory hazard escape sequence
First dragon boss (Level 1 vertical end): first major airborne boss
Level 3-4 transition: Danger boss variation introduces different attack patterns
Final level sequence: combined dragon boss + palace navigation + final boss

notable_level_patterns.description: All five areas follow: (1) top-view vertical-scroll airborne segment with ground-and-air enemies, ending with guardian defeat and palace entry; (2) side-scrolling palace interior with platforming, combat, and boss encounter
Mid-point of vertical segment features giant mechanical face that inhales player, forcing entry into Danger area with unique boss; escape required to continue main path
Hidden treasure areas within vertical segments contain score-boosting chests; no mechanical penalty for missing, pure optional reward for thorough play
NES-exclusive: each vertical segment ends with dragon boss battle before palace entry, extending aerial combat sequences
Weapon upgrades incrementally enable new tactical options; progression: single → twin → penetration → three-way → Turtle Dove; later levels increase power-up item density
